short_description: Windows Event Viewer screenshot showing a Service Control Manager error for the OptiSweep service terminating unexpectedly and an automatic restart action.
detailed_description: This artifact is a screenshot of Windows Event Viewer with the System log selected. An Event Properties dialog for Event 7031 from Service Control Manager is open in the foreground. The visible message states that the OptiSweep service terminated unexpectedly, had done so 1 time, and that corrective action would be taken in 60000 milliseconds by restarting the service. The event details pane shows Level as Error and includes a logged timestamp of 2/20/2026 6:36:02 AM. The surrounding event list contains multiple Service Control Manager entries, mostly informational events, with the error row highlighted.

A foreground dialog titled "Event Properties - Event 7031, Service Control Manager" is open.
The General tab is selected in the Event Properties dialog.
The message text in the dialog states that the OptiSweep service terminated unexpectedly and that corrective action will be taken in 60000 milliseconds to restart the service.
The event metadata visible in the dialog includes Log Name: System, Source: Service Control Manager, Event ID: 7031, Level: Error, User: N/A, OpCode: Info, and Computer: wcsupsfwtxignition.
The logged time visible in the dialog is 2/20/2026 6:36:02 AM.
The background event list shows many Service Control Manager entries, including informational events with Event ID 7036 and one error entry with Event ID 7031.
The right-side actions pane includes options such as Event Properties, Attach Task To This Event, Copy, Save Selected Events, Refresh, and Help.
contextual_interpretation: This screenshot appears to document a Windows service crash or unexpected stop event for the OptiSweep service.
The visible text indicates Windows is configured to attempt an automatic restart after the unexpected termination.
The artifact is useful as diagnostic evidence of the observed service failure condition rather than proof that recovery completed successfully.
